step_0_load.column_quality: all expected columns present, no missing values in checkout_completed or revenue

step_1_srm.interpretation: Randomization is healthy (p=0.81). Split is 50.13% / 49.87%. Safe to proceed to treatment-effect analysis.

step_2_primary.interpretation: Treatment rate (16.80%) is significantly higher than control (15.20%), diff = +1.65 pp (+10.9% relative), p = 0.0446. CI [+0.04 pp, +3.17 pp] excludes zero but barely — the lower bound is 0.04 pp, so the true effect could be much smaller than the point estimate.

aov_completers_only.interpretation: Control AOV ($47.20) is slightly higher than treatment ($45.80), diff = -$1.40 (-3.0% relative), but the two-sided Welch t-test p-value (0.166) is not significant. CI [-$3.39, +$0.58] straddles zero but is lopsided negative — the worst case is a $3.39 decline, which on a $47.20 baseline is -7.2%, violating the 3% guardrail set in the experiment design.

average_order_value.status: AMBIGUOUS — point estimate within tolerance, CI lower bound violates threshold
average_order_value.interpretation: Non-significant effect, but the CI lower bound (-7.2%) materially violates the 3% guardrail. This is a 'not significant does not mean safe' moment — if the worst case of the plausible range is an unacceptable outcome, that is a signal to investigate, not to ship.

support_ticket_rate.interpretation: Support ticket rate rose from 5.17% to 5.51% (+6.5% relative), non-significant (p=0.49). Below the 10% relative threshold from 4.9 — guardrail PASSES. The new checkout flow causes a small uptick in support contacts but within tolerance.

step_4_ewl.reasoning: Primary metric is significant (p=0.045, just at the edge) and support ticket guardrail passes (+6.5% below 10% threshold). But the AOV CI lower bound (-7.2%) materially violates the 3% AOV guardrail even though the test itself is non-significant. This is the 'not significant does not mean safe' case — the worst case of the plausible range is unacceptable.
step_4_ewl.recommendation: LEARN, do not ship yet. Investigate AOV segments (device, platform, signup_days) to determine whether the -7.2% worst-case driver is concentrated in a single segment. If concentrated → ship to unaffected segments. If diffuse → extend experiment to tighten the AOV CI or abort.
